Inner resolve is the steadfast commitment and internal fortitude to persist in your goals or values, especially when faced with adversity, turmoil, or systemic obstacles. It acts as a psychological anchor, providing the "flexible steadiness" needed to remain focused while the "tides of the times rise and fall". So, instead of just looking back at my prior breakthroughs and saying oh yeah I can get through this current one because I got through this or that in the past, I needed inner resolve in the current moment so that my pain didn't turn into suffering. You see pain is inevitable, but suffering is optional. Pain is inevitable because it is characteristic of life, while suffering is optional because it is a reaction to pain. Suffering comes only after pain has been experienced in life but once suffering is established it no longer needs pain to keep it going or growing. Pain is dependent on life while suffering can become self-sufficient. You seeCLICK | Pain is a person’s experience of emotional or psychological discomfort. I made it to the top of that mountain then realized I had to jump off that motherfucker to save myself. As I was falling from my emergency jump, I realized that mountain wasn't neccessary, it was a choice. Life is a journey and there are many paths you can choose to follow and each of those paths have different terrains you'll encounter. Other examples are valleys, hills, plains, deserts, plateaus, canyons, and even different bodies of water. Each type has unique features that influence your experience. As I mentioned before, so long as your life continues, so does your journey no matter what you've accomplished. Since the journey continues, pain is ineviatable but suffering remains a sizeable option. Inner resolve is what opts you out of suffering.
